title: Bank of England Weekly Report 8 April 2026
summary: The Bank of England published its weekly balance sheet data for the week ending 8 April 2026, showing reserves balances at £650,510mn, up from £645,085mn the prior week. The report contains routine mechanical data on the Bank's sterling liabilities, sterling assets, foreign currency liabilities, and foreign currency assets with no accompanying commentary or policy signals.
key_points: Reserves balances increased to £650,510mn from £645,085mn week-over-week
Short-term repo operations increased to £100,301mn from £97,441mn
Indexed long-term repo increased to £74,183mn from £72,774mn
Loan to Asset Purchase Facility unchanged at £553,158mn
Term Funding Scheme with additional incentives for SMEs unchanged at £41,894mn
Foreign currency reserve assets decreased to £16,368mn from £17,820mn
why_it_matters: This is the Bank of England's routine weekly balance sheet disclosure providing transparency into BoE operations including reserve levels, repo operations, and asset holdings. The report contains no commentary, analysis, or policy signals—it is mechanical data only. The source is thin on substantive content and would be most useful for tracking operational trends rather than capturing policy thinking.
market_structure_context: The report provides data on the BoE's operational framework including short-term repo operations (£100,301mn), indexed long-term repo (£74,183mn), and the Term Funding Scheme (£41,894mn). These operations inform understanding of BoE liquidity management but carry no policy signal in this purely mechanical release.
important_numbers: £41,894mn Term Funding Scheme with additional incentives for SMEs
